perturbation.original_text: Failure of Company to comply with the provisions of this Section 2(b) shall entitle Pey Dirt to revoke this license immediately (notwithstanding any other provision in this Agreement to the contrary).
perturbation.changed_text: Failure of Company to comply with the provisions of this Section 2(b) shall entitle Pey Dirt to revoke this license, but only after providing 90 days written notice to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. The original text allowed Pey Dirt to revoke the license immediately upon the Company's failure to actively promote Endorsed Products or prevent their sale outside the Contract Territory. The modified text requires a 90-day written notice before revocation. This contradicts the 'immediately' clause and introduces uncertainty about the revocation process. This contradicts the original clause within the section itself and will affect enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Company agrees that during the Contract Period it will use its best efforts actively and aggressively to (i) promote the sale of all Endorsed Products in the Contract Territory, and (ii) prevent the sale of any Endorsed Products outside the Contract Territory. Failure of Company to comply with the provisions of this Section 2(b) shall entitle Pey Dirt to revoke this license, but only after providing 90 days written notice to the Company. (notwithstanding any other provision in this Agreement to the contrary).
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for immediate revocation of the license upon failure to comply with Section 2(b). The changed text modifies this by requiring a 90-day written notice before revocation. This directly contradicts the original 'immediately' clause, creating a structural flaw and uncertainty about the revocation process. The contradiction is strong, as it changes the fundamental timing of the revocation, making the two sections incompatible. The contradiction is within the section itself, impacting its enforceability and creating a clear conflict between the original and modified text.

perturbation.original_text: From and after the termination of the Contract Period all of the rights of Company to the use of Manning Identification shall cease absolutely and Company shall not thereafter use or refer to Manning Identification in advertising or promotion in any manner whatsoever, it being understood by Company that Manning Identification may be used at any time by others in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Products the shipment of which is completed after the termination of the Contract Period.
perturbation.changed_text: From and after the termination of the Contract Period all of the rights of Company to the use of Manning Identification shall cease absolutely, however, the company may continue to use the Manning Identification for a period of 180 days to sell off pre-existing inventory. Company shall not thereafter use or refer to Manning Identification in advertising or promotion in any manner whatsoever, it being understood by Company that Manning Identification may be used at any time by others in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Products the shipment of which is completed after the termination of the Contract Period.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'however, the company may continue to use the Manning Identification for a period of 180 days to sell off pre-existing inventory' directly contradicts the clause that 'all of the rights of Company to the use of Manning Identification shall cease absolutely'. This creates an in-text contradiction, and the uncertainty regarding the company's rights after contract termination. This directly affects the enforcement of the termination clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From and after the termination of the Contract Period all of the rights of Company to the use of Manning Identification shall cease absolutely, however, the company may continue to use the Manning Identification for a period of 180 days to sell off pre-existing inventory. Company shall not thereafter use or refer to Manning Identification in advertising or promotion in any manner whatsoever, it being understood by Company that Manning Identification may be used at any time by others in connection with the advertisement and promotion of Products the shipment of which is completed after the termination of the Contract Period. It is further agreed that following termination of the Contract Period, Company shall not advertise, promote, distribute or sell any item whatsoever in connection with the use of any name, figure, design, logo, trademark or trade name similar to or suggestive of Manning Identification.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception allowing the company to use the Manning Identification for 180 days after termination, which directly contradicts the original statement that 'all of the rights of Company to the use of Manning Identification shall cease absolutely'. Since the changed text and contradicted text are in the same location, this demonstrates a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction as outlined in the definition for 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Under no circumstances will Manning or Pey Dirt, on the one hand, or Company, on the other hand, be liable to the other or any other entity for any special, consequential, indirect, exemplary and/or punitive damages, or for loss of good will or business profits.
perturbation.changed_text: Under no circumstances will Manning or Pey Dirt, on the one hand, or Company, on the other hand, be liable to the other or any other entity for any special, consequential, indirect, exemplary and/or punitive damages, or for loss of good will or business profits, unless it's proven in court, that the liable party did so with gross negligence.
perturbation.explanation: The original text is a blanket waiver of special, consequential, indirect, exemplary, and/or punitive damages, and for loss of good will or business profits. By adding 'unless it's proven in court, that the liable party did so with gross negligence', this introduces an exception to the waiver. This directly contradicts the 'Under no circumstances' wording, creating uncertainty as to when this waiver is actually applicable. This new exception creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In no event (including, but not limited to, Manning's or Pey Dirt's default hereunder) shall Manning or Pey Dirt be liable to Company (or any entity claiming through Company) for any amount in excess of the amounts of royalties actually received by Pey Dirt hereunder, excluding the reimbursement of expenses. Under no circumstances will Manning or Pey Dirt, on the one hand, or Company, on the other hand, be liable to the other or any other entity for any special, consequential, indirect, exemplary and/or punitive damages, or for loss of good will or business profits, unless it's proven in court, that the liable party did so with gross negligence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the blanket waiver of liability for certain damages ('unless it's proven in court, that the liable party did so with gross negligence'). This directly contradicts the 'Under no circumstances' wording in both the changed text and the contradicted text. The contradictory language creates uncertainty about when the waiver applies, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
